# Kiro vs. `claude -p` Haiku — paired fidelity check (2026-06-16)

**Question.** The E0-final view set is generated by **Claude Haiku 4.5** via two
delivery paths: `claude -p` under OAuth (426 views: 253 local + 173 over SSH to
a second OAuth host) and the **Kiro CLI** headless mode (`kiro-cli chat --no-interactive
--model claude-haiku-4.5`, credit-metered, third-party-proxied) for the
remainder. Before declaring the gate **non-provisional**, confirm Kiro's
`claude-haiku-4.5` behaves like the first-party Haiku — otherwise a
generator-mix style difference (orthogonal to position) would depress the Tier-B
probe and bias the regime call toward *signal-carrying*.

**Method.** `kiro_fidelity_check.py --n 8`: take 8 (doc_id,k) pairs already
compressed via the OAuth path, re-compress the *same* slice through Kiro, compare
paired. Deterministic slice selection (spread across docs/positions). ~0.4 credits.

**Result (n=8).**

| metric | OAuth `claude -p` | Kiro `claude-haiku-4.5` |
|---|---|---|
| mean words | 390 | 372 |
| mean compression ratio (tiktoken) | 7.4 | 7.0 |
| source-number recall | 0.38 | 0.52 |
| summary↔summary content-word jaccard | — | 0.46 |

**Read.** Length and compression ratio are statistically indistinguishable; both
land in the ~358-word target band. Number retention is comparable (Kiro slightly
higher). The 0.46 content-word overlap between the two summaries is the expected
level for two *abstractive* summaries of identical input — surface form varies,
content tracks — not the near-identity that would signal caching nor the
near-disjointness that would signal a different/weaker model.

**Conclusion.** Kiro's `claude-haiku-4.5` is a faithful stand-in for the
first-party Haiku for this summarization task. The mixed-path E0-final dataset is
treated as a single **Claude Haiku 4.5** compressor (D29/D30). Residual
path-to-path style variance is orthogonal to within-doc position (generator
correlates with doc-id range, not with k), so it cannot manufacture a spurious
position signal; at most it adds bounded noise. Provenance is preserved per-record
via `gen_path` (`local`/`remote`/`kiro`) to allow a path-restricted robustness
re-run of the gate.
